news.title: Pace of work slow despite NBCC takeover, say Supertech homebuyers
news.para: Supertech Ltd launched several residential and commercial projects in Noida, Greater Noida, the Yamuna Expressway region, Gurgaon and other cities, mostly between 2010 and 2012. Delays in completing these projects led to defaults on repayments to financial creditors.

news.title: Gurugram: 13 years on, no handover, Haryana RERA orders ₹50 lakh compensation to buyer
news.para: Haryana Real Estate Regulatory Authority ordered a developer to pay compensation. The homebuyer booked a unit in 2010, with possession due in 2012. The project remained incomplete for over six years past the promised delivery date. The authority awarded compensation for delay and mental harassment. The developer must also pay litigation costs and interest on the awarded sum.

news.title: Bengaluru builder to raze shaky tower three months after getting occupancy certificate
news.para: An 18-floor apartment tower in Bengaluru will be demolished soon. This decision comes after the building was found to be tilting significantly. The builder plans to reconstruct the entire 49-unit tower within two years. Buyers will have to wait longer for their dream homes. Authorities are reviewing the occupancy certificate and ensuring nearby structures remain safe.

news.title: Odisha REAT dismisses promoter’s appeal against ORERA order
news.para: The Odisha Real Estate Appellate Tribunal dismissed a promoter's appeal against an ORERA order.  The tribunal upheld directions to protect apartment owners' interests in a residential project.  Deficiencies in promised amenities and structural issues were noted during inspections.  The promoter must rectify defects and complete all formalities within three months.  Common areas will be maintained by the promoter until formal handover to the association.

news.title: Finolex shares row: NCLAT upheld NCLT's order allowing amendment in nine-year-old company petition
news.para: The NCLAT dismissed Prakash Chhabria's appeals against an NCLT order. This order allowed his brother Deepak Chhabria to amend a company petition. The appellate tribunal upheld the NCLT's decision to permit amendments. It kept the question of limitation open for the final hearing. The NCLT will now decide the merits of the amended pleadings.

news.title: UltraTech Cement plans to raise ₹5,000 crore from market
news.para: UltraTech Cement plans to raise Rs 5,000 crore through non-convertible debentures. The company's finance committee approved this proposal for private placement issuance. This fundraising aims to support its business operations and expansion plans. Earlier, the company reported a significant jump in its June quarter net profit. UltraTech Cement also intends to enter the cables and wires business this fiscal year.

news.title: Central government reorganises housing and urban affairs ministry
news.para: Under the new arrangement, the Department of Capital Development will handle functions including oversight of CPWD, DDA, DMRC, government estates, Union government buildings, land acquisition and development in the National Capital Territory and planning for the National Capital Region (NCR).

news.title: SC defers hearing on plea seeking refund for homebuyers in Supertech twin towers case
news.para: The Supreme Court has postponed a hearing on Supertech twin-towers homebuyers' refund plea. Efforts are underway to satisfy claims for those not opting for alternative allotments. The court granted three months to explore further options for homebuyers' satisfaction. Homebuyers are not receiving refunds, with last payments made in 2024. The twin-towers in Noida were demolished on August 28, 2022.

news.title: Mahindra Lifespaces' net profit rises 67% to ₹85.55 crore in Q1 FY27
news.para: Mahindra Lifespaces' consolidated total income increased to ₹977.54 crore during the quarter. Residential pre-sales more than doubled to ₹925 crore, while gross development value additions stood at ₹5,600 crore.

news.title: Knowledge Realty Trust revenue up over 16%, EBITDA & NOI rise 18% since IPO: CEO
news.para: Knowledge Realty Trust has recorded over 16% revenue growth and 18% growth in EBITDA and net operating income since its IPO, said Shirish Godbole, CEO, Knowledge Realty Trust. The REIT has 92% occupancy, 2.6 million sq ft under development and 18% leverage, which it plans to use for future acquisitions.

news.title: Five-member panel to probe illegal transfer of 356 property tax accounts in Indore
news.para: Indore Municipal Corporation is investigating 356 illegal property tax account transfers. A five-member committee of officials and IT experts is leading the probe. Unauthorized ownership changes occurred using municipal officials' login credentials previously. All 356 illegal transfers have been cancelled, reverting records to original owners. Criminal charges and FIRs will be filed based on the committee's findings.

news.title: Civic bodies in Haryana face heat as SC reviews enforcement against illegal structures
news.para: Supreme Court sharply escalated its scrutiny of urban planning violations in Gurugram.  MCG and GMDA officials face consequences if demolition orders remain unenforced.  The apex court ordered personal appearances of key officials with enforcement reports.  Authorities are criticized for face-saving exercises and lack of accountability for erring officers.  Future reports must identify disciplinary actions taken against responsible officials.

news.title: Cops begin drive to verify tenants in Bokaro
news.para: Bokaro police initiated a tenant verification campaign across Sector police station limits. This drive aims to collect tenant details and enhance surveillance for crime prevention. Police personnel are conducting door-to-door visits to gather essential information from residents. Landlords are urged to cooperate with the verification process at Sector-4 police station. Public participation is crucial for identifying suspicious elements and maintaining city security.

news.title: UP housing board initiates process to demolish 44 sealed properties in Meerut
news.para: Uttar Pradesh Housing Board will demolish forty-four sealed properties in Meerut.  This action follows a Supreme Court order issued on July fourteenth.  Property owners will receive a fifteen-day notice before any demolition occurs.  Traders express deep concern over potential livelihood impacts and business disruptions.  Appeals have been made for humane solutions respecting the court's directive.

news.title: Fresh PMAY-Gramin house approvals plunge sharply in Andhra Pradesh
news.para: New Pradhan Mantri Awaas Yojana-Gramin sanctions in Andhra Pradesh have virtually halted after 2022-23.  Only 515 rural houses received new approvals across three financial years.  Construction of previously sanctioned houses continues at a modest pace.  Central funding releases also saw a sharp decline during this period.  Overall, the scheme's progress in the state reflects these recent trends.

news.title: Jaipur metro phase-II project construction begins
news.para: Rajasthan Chief Minister Bhajanlal Sharma initiated the Jaipur Metro Phase-2 project. This expansion aims to enhance urban connectivity within the state capital. The nearly 41-kilometer corridor will link key industrial and residential areas. The project, costing over Rs 13,037 crore, connects vital city locations. Civil work has commenced swiftly after the virtual foundation laying ceremony.

news.title: Nagpur: Kanhan Metro may redraw skyline, TOD to unlock development along belt
news.para: The Kanhan Metro extension will enable denser construction and commercial development.  Transit-Oriented Development will concentrate growth around the new Metro stations.  Authorities may permit additional floor space index after collecting a premium.  Revenue generation will support loan repayment and future infrastructure works.  This extension aims for completion by December 2027.

news.title: FIR against Ansal Group, four others for ₹43 lakh plot fraud
news.para: Police filed a case against Ansal Group and four individuals for alleged fraud. Two buyers claim they were cheated out of Rs 43 lakh for residential plots. The buyers purchased plots from Dr Narayan Prasad and his wife Renu. They later discovered issues selling the plots without Ansal's NOC. The complainants accused the Ansal owners of conspiracy and threats.
